findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_settings" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the authenticated user's Meta Council settings — preferred model, plan tier, and which provider/tool API keys are configured (booleans only, never the secre…

name: get_agent_detail
description: Full detail on one expert agent by slug (from list_agents): role, description, default model, domain, tags, and tools.

name: advance_workflow
description: Advance a workflow past a human checkpoint — approve or reject the paused step so the pipeline continues. Use the session id from run_workflow / get_workflow_session. Requires authentication.

name: get_settings
description: Get the authenticated user's Meta Council settings — preferred model, plan tier, and which provider/tool API keys are configured (booleans only, never the secret values). Requires authentication.

name: list_outreach_campaigns
description: List the authenticated user's outreach campaigns with live lead / sent / reply counts. Requires authentication.

name: search_outreach_leads
description: Search the user's outreach leads — filter by a text query (company / contact / email), pipeline status, and/or campaign. Returns company, contact, status, pitch, and reply info. Requires authentication.

name: outreach_analytics
description: Outreach summary for the user — total leads, sent, replied, and reply rate. Requires authentication.

name: score_locus_case
description: Score an anonymized adult mental-health / addiction case against LOCUS. Convenes the LOCUS Assessment Panel (psychiatrist, addiction specialist, clinical social worker, utilization reviewer, peer specialist, safety officer); each reviewer independently rates all six LOCUS dimensions, then a DETERMIN

name: locus_determine_from_scores
description: Compute a LOCUS Level of Care from dimension ratings you already have. Deterministic — no LLM, instant: composite + Determination Grid + the inviolable override floors (e.g. Risk-of-Harm=4 → minimum Level 5), applied in code. Provide EITHER the seven flat D* ratings (1-5 each; Dimension IV splits in

name: campaign_pipeline_stats
description: Pipeline-stage breakdown for one outreach campaign — total / sent / active / won / lost, reply rate, and per-stage lead counts. Requires authentication.

name: list_campaign_triggers
description: List automation triggers (auto-reply rules, status updates, notifications) configured for an outreach campaign. Requires authentication.

name: list_campaign_replies
description: List sent / received emails for an outreach campaign, newest first — subject, body preview, and reply classification. Requires authentication.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_visualization, search_outreach_leads, list_campaign_triggers, list_campaign_replies · Private data: get_settings, search_outreach_leads · Public sink: run_council, search_outreach_leads · Destructive: run_council, list_workflows, run_workflow, get_workflow_session.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_visualization, search_outreach_leads, list_campaign_triggers, list_campaign_replies · Private data: get_settings, search_outreach_leads · Public sink: run_council, search_outreach_leads · Destructive: run_council, list_workflows, run_workflow, get_workflow_session

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
